text.title: SDD Workflows Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding SDD Workflows. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every workflow has a stable name (spec-workflow, design-workflow, …) referenced from manifests.
rule.rationale: Every workflow has a stable name (spec-workflow, design-workflow, …) referenced from manifests.

rule.statement: Workflow definition lists typed input and output artefacts; untyped is invalid.
rule.rationale: Workflow definition lists typed input and output artefacts; untyped is invalid.

rule.statement: Every workflow names the agents/models involved per step.
rule.rationale: Every workflow names the agents/models involved per step.

rule.statement: Workflow closes when its associated gate emits pass; no implicit close.
rule.rationale: Workflow closes when its associated gate emits pass; no implicit close.

rule.statement: Workflows do not write to each other's output paths; cross-phase hand-off goes through artefacts.
rule.rationale: Workflows do not write to each other's output paths; cross-phase hand-off goes through artefacts.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-workflows.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
